_meta._doc: Per-metric ceilings. CI's quality-ratchet workflow fails if any current value EXCEEDS the baseline (regressions). Lower-is-better; values can drop freely and the workflow auto-suggests updating this file when it sees a sustained improvement.
_meta._update_policy: Manual. Lower values mean 'we improved'. The author of an improvement-PR may update this file in the same commit; reviewers verify the new baseline matches the actual measurement (see scripts/capture-quality-baselines.sh). Never raise a value to silence a new finding — fix the finding first.
_meta._argv_doc: Each entry's `argv` is the exact argv passed to subprocess.run with shell=False. Splitting at JSON level (vs `shell=True` on a single command string) keeps the tool-spawn surface free of shell-injection vectors per CWE-78.

ruff.note: Total errors. Most are auto-fixable via `ruff check --fix` — that's a free PR worth landing.

mypy_strict.note: Tracked weekly in tests/quality/type-coverage-progress.md. Drops as each Phase 1-6 PR lands.

pylint_score.note: Higher-is-better. Scoped to ERROR + FATAL severities only (--enable=E,F); style rules excluded since ruff covers them. Operator override of 'gte' inverts the comparison.

eslint.note: Must stay at 0 — CLAUDE.md invariant. Any non-zero turns CI red regardless of baseline.

semgrep.note: Must stay at 0. Same invariant as eslint — never weaken; fix the finding.

_mutmut_baselines._doc: Mutmut runs are too slow (~5min/file, leadhunter ~20min) for the PR-time quality-ratchet. The weekly `.github/workflows/mutation-test.yml` job is the gate; this block exists only to document the achieved floor when the baseline was first executed on 2026-05-23, so future drops below these numbers are visible in `git blame`. Per-file mutmut command lives in `tests/quality/mutation-kill-rates.md`.
_mutmut_baselines._threshold: Workflow MIN_KILL_RATE=80 is the actual gate. Leadhunter is intentionally excluded — see scope-mismatch note in the report.
